title: run fails with error creating cgroup directory
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman run ubuntu:18.04

**Describe the results you received:**
```
Error: creating cgroup directory `/sys/fs/cgroup/cpuset/user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/user.slice/libpod-bb607f8075bbc8252f8eeea06e4bb1ed684287d8ef9a00a7c18547ee7c93490d.scope`: No such file or directory: OCI runtime command not found error
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

The container should be launched.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 1.8.2
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL

      commit: bbd6f25c70d5db2a1cd3bfb0416a8db99a75ed7e
  uptime: 48h 22m 21.68s (Approximately 2.00 days)
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-1.8.2-2.fc31.x86_64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
physical

I also tried running with sudo, that failed with a slightly different message:
```
$ sudo podman run ubuntu:18.04
Error: open '/sys/fs/cgroup/cpuset/machine.slice/libpod-9711fec094819e210881a9c9dc120ffca623cd9f20fa1c68ce1a1b35ea514f62.scope/cpuset.cpus': No such file or directory: OCI runtime command not found error
```

comments.body:
how does your `/sys/fs/cgroup` look like?

Can you show the output for `ls /sys/fs/cgroup` and `findmnt -R /sys/fs/cgroup` ?

```
$ ls /sys/fs/cgroup
cgroup.controllers	cgroup.stat		cpuset	       io.cost.qos	system.slice
cgroup.max.depth	cgroup.subtree_control	cros	       io.pressure	user.slice
cgroup.max.descendants	cgroup.threads		init.scope     machine.slice
cgroup.procs		cpu.pressure		io.cost.model  memory.pressure
```

```
$ findmnt -R /sys/fs/cgroup
TARGET                SOURCE  FSTYPE  OPTIONS
/sys/fs/cgroup        cgroup2 cgroup2 rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime,seclabel,nsdelegate
└─/sys/fs/cgroup/cros cros    cgroup  rw,relatime,seclabel,cpuset
```

what is cros?  How is the `/sys/fs/cgroup/cros` mount created?

The cpuset controller is mounted on `/sys/fs/cgroup/cros` as cgroup v1 so it is not usable by cgroup v2.

This configuration is not supported. 

We support either the legacy cgroup v1 or the unified cgroup v2 mode: https://systemd.io/CGROUP_DELEGATION/#three-different-tree-setups-

Also the configuration above is not recognized as `hybrid` as it creates some non-standard mounts (for hybrid mode, `/sys/fs/cgroup` should be a tmpfs).

The cros stuff is created by the `cros_sdk` tool used for building ChromiumOS and its derivatives: https://chromium.googlesource.com/chromiumos/docs/+/master/developer_guide.md#building-chromium-os

Thanks for taking a look and providing this info. I didn't realize these tools could interfere with each other. Sounds like if there is a bug here it's on the cros side, not podman, so I'll close this.
